name: Bruscetta 1
recipeIngredient: 1 loaf French bread, the fresher the better
1/4 cup olive oil, the kind with some flavor
6 Roma tomatoes, a.k.a. plum or Italian tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the bread lengthwise once and cut each half in half shortways. Arrange the four pieces on a cookie sheet or foil.
2. Process the garlic and olive oil in a mini chopper until fully blended (15 seconds). Brush this mixture onto the open faces of all four bread pieces.
3. Slice the tomatoes into rounds, about silver dollar size, and spread them evenly over the bread.
4. Sprinkle the oregano and basil evenly over each piece of bread.
5. Sprinkle the Parmesan cheese evenly over each piece as well.
6. Place the cookie sheet or foil under the broiler in the oven.
7. Cook for 5-15 minutes, or until the cheese melts and the edges of the bread just start to brown. Turn off the heat when finished.
